questions.question: Which statement best describes the purpose of science?
options: To explain the natural world using evidence
To prove that old ideas are always wrong
To make guesses without testing them
questions.answer: To explain the natural world using evidence
questions.feedback: Science uses evidence from observations and investigations to explain the natural world.

questions.question: Which of these is most important when scientists make a claim?
questions.feedback: Scientific claims should be supported by evidence.

questions.question: Why do scientists repeat investigations?
options: To check whether results are reliable
questions.answer: To check whether results are reliable
questions.feedback: Repeating investigations helps scientists check the reliability of their results.

questions.question: What is a fair test?
options: An investigation where only one variable is changed at a time
An investigation where everything is changed
An investigation based only on guesses
questions.answer: An investigation where only one variable is changed at a time
questions.feedback: A fair test changes one variable and keeps other conditions controlled.

questions.question: Which object is at the centre of our Solar System?
questions.feedback: The Sun is the star at the centre of our Solar System.

questions.question: What causes day and night on Earth?
options: Earth moving closer to the Sun
questions.feedback: Day and night are caused by Earth rotating on its axis.

questions.question: Why are Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander astronomical knowledges important in science learning?
options: They show long-term observations of the sky and environment
They are only stories with no patterns
questions.answer: They show long-term observations of the sky and environment
questions.feedback: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander astronomical knowledges include careful observations of sky patterns and environmental changes.

questions.question: Which is an example of using sky observations in everyday life?
options: Using star patterns to support navigation or seasonal knowledge
Ignoring patterns in the night sky
Changing the length of a day
questions.answer: Using star patterns to support navigation or seasonal knowledge
questions.feedback: Sky observations can support navigation, seasonal knowledge and understanding environmental patterns.

questions.question: Why have models of the Universe changed over time?
options: New evidence and better technology have improved our understanding
questions.answer: New evidence and better technology have improved our understanding
questions.feedback: Scientific models can change when new evidence or better technology improves our understanding.

questions.question: Which tool has helped scientists observe distant objects in space?
questions.feedback: Telescopes help scientists observe distant objects in space.
